result.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: 0.881
JUSTIFICATION: In the mean-field approximation for a lattice gas, the occupancy <n> is given by the Fermi-Dirac-like distribution: <n> = 1 / (1 + exp(-(mu - E_mf) / (k_B T))). Given the parameters, the effective energy term simplifies such that the exponent is -(6/pi + 0.1). Calculating <n> = 1 / (1 + exp(-6/pi - 0.1)) yields approximately 0.881.
